policy_name: Maternal Serum Marker Prenatal Screening Order Policy
purpose: This policy describes how <<LAB_NAME>> accepts and processes orders for maternal serum marker prenatal screening (first-trimester combined, second-trimester quad, integrated, etc.). Required by 42 CFR 493.1232 (specimen integrity), 42 CFR 493.1241 (test request), and 42 CFR 493.1291 (test report).

cfr_text_blocks.label: Standard: Test request content (includes additional required info)
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: The laboratory must ensure the test request solicits all of the following information: (1) The name and address or other suitable identifiers of the authorized person requesting the test. (2) The patient's name or unique patient identifier. (3) The sex and age or date of birth of the patient. (4) The test(s) to be performed. (5) The source of the specimen, when appropriate. (6) The date and, if appropriate, time of specimen collection. (8) Any additional information relevant and necessary for a specific test to ensure accurate and timely testing and reporting of results, including interpretation, if applicable.

scope: All <<LAB_NAME>> personnel who accept maternal serum marker screening orders and the medical director or designee.
policy_statements: Every maternal serum marker order includes: patient identifiers, gestational age (determined by ultrasound or LMP), maternal weight, maternal date of birth, race or ethnicity (if used in the risk algorithm), diabetic status, smoking status, singleton vs multiple gestation, and prior history of neural tube defect or chromosomal abnormality.
Orders missing required clinical context for accurate risk calculation are not accepted; the ordering provider is contacted for the missing information before testing.

Results are reported with the calculated risk, the reference ranges used, the gestational age basis (ultrasound or LMP), and any limitations of the screening (false-positive and false-negative rate, follow-up recommendations).
All such results are released only after review by the technical supervisor or medical director or designee per 42 CFR 493.1445/1451 for the specialty.
procedure_steps: Order receipt staff verify all required clinical elements are present; missing elements trigger ordering provider contact before testing.
Specimens are tested per the manufacturer's instructions and the validated protocol.
Results are calculated per the risk algorithm with the lab's verified performance specifications; the technical supervisor or medical director or designee reviews before release.
Reports include the calculated risk, gestational age basis, reference ranges, and follow-up recommendations.
definitions: Risk-calculation test combining biochemical markers and clinical context to estimate the probability of fetal chromosomal abnormality or neural tube defect.
The data source used to set gestational age in the risk calculation: ultrasound (preferred when available) or last menstrual period (LMP).
